content.text: I need a way to dynamically determine a standardized name for a method based on class properties, suitable for use in the Visitor pattern.
What happens if the `self.TYPE` attribute exists but is explicitly set to None?
If `self.TYPE` is `None`, calling `.replace()` will raise an error, which triggers the generic exception handler and causes the function to return the lowercase name of the class instead.
That clarifies how the fallback mechanism works!
